title: Ricinus globosus
intro:
I couldn't find any information on a plant called "Ricinus globosus". However, I found that Ricinus communis, also known as the castor oil plant, is a species of flowering plant in the spurge family. 

The castor oil plant (Ricinus communis) is a plant with a large, palmate leaf and a seed that produces a valuable oil. The health benefits of castor oil include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can help with skin conditions, wound healing, and digestive issues. The bioactive constituents of castor oil include ricinoleic acid, oleic acid, and ricinine, which contribute to its therapeutic properties. The most common herbal preparations made with castor oil include topical creams and ointments for skin conditions and massage oil for pain relief.
taxonomy: Ricinus globosus is a species within the genus Ricinus. This genus belongs to the family Euphorbiaceae. The family Euphorbiaceae is a part of the larger order Malpighiales. Malpighiales is a subgroup within the clade Rosids, which is a division of the larger clade Angiosperms. Angiosperms, also known as flowering plants, are a major group of plants in the kingdom Plantae.
uses: Ricinus globosus is not a commonly discussed plant. However, its close relative, Ricinus communis, is known for its medicinal properties. Ricinus communis has been used in traditional medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, particularly in treating skin conditions and wounds. It also contains ricin, which has been researched for its potential anti-cancer properties. Further research is needed to confirm the medicinal uses of Ricinus globosus.
benefits: Ricinus globosus is often confused with Ricinus communis, but there are some differences between the two species. However, Ricinus communis has been reported to have potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties. Ricinus communis seeds contain ricin, which can be used as a potential treatment for certain types of cancer. More research is needed to fully understand the health benefits of Ricinus communis, but some studies suggest it may also have antiviral and antibacterial properties. Additional studies on Ricinus globosus would be necessary to confirm its potential health benefits.
constituents: Ricinus globosus is actually a synonym for Ricinus communis, more commonly known as the castor oil plant. Ricinus globosus.  The active constituents of Ricinus communis include ricin, ricinine, and ricinoleic acid, a monounsaturated fatty acid with anti-inflammatory properties. Ricinoleic acid is responsible for the medicinal and industrial applications of castor oil. This oil is also used as a laxative and for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. It is extracted from the seeds of the plant and used in various pharmaceutical and industrial applications.
parts: Ricinus globosus is actually another name for the castor oil plant, Ricinus communis, not Ricinus globosus, which is not recognized.
